back_translation: By reason of the fact that many have attempted to compose a narrative regarding the events which have been fulfilled among us all,
just as those who were eyewitnesses [witnesses seen with eye] and servants of the divine word from the beginning have handed it down to us,
therefore, I [humble-self] have seen it fitting to write to you [formal] in order, carefully, after I have investigated every matter thoroughly from the outset, most honored Theophilus,
so that you [formal] may come to know the certainty of the various words which you [formal] have received instruction in.
In the reign of King Herod of the region of Judea, there was a certain priest named Zechariah, from the priest-group of Abijah; his wife was a descendant of Aaron, named Elizabeth.
Both were righteous persons before the face [royal] of God [royal], conducting their life according to all the commands [royal] and regulations of the Lord [royal] blamelessly.
But both had no children, because Elizabeth was barren, and both had aged into their elderly years already.
It came to pass one day, while Zechariah was carrying out priestly duties before the face [royal] of God [royal] in the rotation of his group,
according to the custom of the priesthood, he was chosen by casting lots to enter and burn fragrant incense in the temple [royal] of the Lord [royal].
At the time of the incense offering, all the people were praying outside.
Suddenly, an angel of the Lord [royal] appeared to Zechariah, standing at the right side of the altar for burning incense.
When Zechariah saw the angel, he was startled, and fear came over him.
But the angel said to him, 'Do not be afraid, Zechariah, because your prayer has been heard [royal-passive]. Elizabeth your wife will bear you a son, and you shall name him John.
You yourself will have great delight and exultation, and many will rejoice because of his birth,

for he will be a great one in the eye-sight [royal] of the Lord [royal]. He will not drink wine or any strong liquor at all, and will be filled with the Holy Spirit [royal] from while he is in his mother's womb.
And he will cause many of the people of Israel to turn back to the Lord [royal] their God.

He himself will go ahead before the face [royal] of Him [royal] with the spirit and mighty power of Elijah, to cause the hearts of ancestors to turn back to their descendants, and to cause the disobedient to turn back to the wisdom of the righteous, in order to prepare a ready people for the Lord [royal].'
Zechariah said to the angel, 'How will I [humble-self] know that this is true? For I [humble-self] myself am an aged man, and my wife also has aged into her elderly years already.'
The angel answered him, 'I [dignified] am Gabriel, the one who stands before the face [royal] of God [royal], and I have been sent to speak with you and to proclaim this good news to you.
And behold, you will be mute and unable to speak until the day when these events will occur, because you did not believe my words, which those words will be fulfilled in their time.'
Meanwhile, the people were waiting for Zechariah, and were surprised that he was delayed in the temple.
When he came out, he was unable to speak with them, and they realized that he had seen a vision in the temple. He could only make signs to them but still could not speak.
When the days of his duty had been completed, he then returned to his own home.
After those days, Elizabeth his wife became pregnant, and she withdrew herself in quiet seclusion for five months. She said,
'The Lord [royal] has done [royal] this for me [humble-self] in these days, when He has looked [royal-see] upon me [humble-self] to remove my [humble-self] disgrace among people.'
In that sixth month, God [royal] sent the angel Gabriel [royal-send] to a town in the region of Galilee called Nazareth,
to a virgin who was betrothed to a man named Joseph, who was a descendant of David. That virgin was named Mary.
The angel came in to her and said, 'Rejoice, O woman who has been greatly graced. The Lord [royal] dwells [royal] with you [formal-you].'
She was deeply startled when she heard those words, and pondered what this greeting might mean.
The angel therefore said to her, 'Do not be afraid, Mary, for you [formal] have received grace from God.
Behold, you [formal] will conceive and give birth to a son, and you [formal] shall name him Jesus.
This son will be a great one, and will be named the Son [royal] of the Most High. The Lord [royal] God will grant [royal-give] to Him [royal] the royal throne of David His [royal] ancestor.
He [royal] will reign [royal] over the clan of Jacob continuing forever, and His [royal] kingdom will have no end at all.'
Mary then said to the angel, 'How can this thing be, since I [polite-fem] have not yet known-in-union with any man?'

The angel answered her, 'The Holy Spirit [royal] will descend [royal] upon you [formal], and the mighty power of the Most High will overshadow [royal] you [formal] with shadow. Therefore, the Holy One who will be born [royal-born] will be called the Son [royal] of God.
And behold, Elizabeth your [formal] own relative has also conceived a son in her aged state. Now she who is called a barren woman is currently pregnant in her sixth month.
For no thing is impossible for God.'
Mary answered, 'I [humble-self] am a female servant-slave of the Lord [royal]. May it happen to me [humble-self] according to your words.' Then the angel departed from her.
At that time, Mary rose up and traveled to a town in the hilly area of Judah in a hurry.
She entered the house of Zechariah and greeted Elizabeth.
When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby in her womb stirred, and Elizabeth became filled with the Holy Spirit [royal].
She let out a loud voice, proclaiming, 'You [formal] have received blessing among all women, and the one in your [formal] womb has also received blessing.
Why indeed have I [humble-self] received this honor — that the mother of the Lord [royal] of mine [humble-self] has come [royal] to me [humble-self]?
For behold, when the sound of your [formal] greeting reached my [humble-self] ear, the baby in my [humble-self] womb stirred with joy.
The one who believed is blessed [happy], because what the Lord [royal] has spoken [royal] to her will be fulfilled completely.'
Mary then said, 'My [humble-self] spirit-soul extols the Lord [royal],
and my [humble-self] spirit rejoices exultantly in God my Savior,

because He [royal] has looked-upon [royal] the lowly status of the female servant-slave of His [royal]. Behold, from this time onward, all generations of people will call me [humble-self] blessed [happy],
because the Mighty One has done [royal] great things for me [humble-self]. His [royal] name is holy [royal].
His mercy [royal] extends to those who revere-fear Him [royal] throughout every generation.
He [royal] has demonstrated [royal] mighty power with His [royal] arm [royal]; He has scattered [royal] those who are arrogantly proud in the thoughts within their heart.
He has cast down [royal] rulers from their thrones, but He has raised up [royal] those of low status to become high.
He has given [royal] the hungry to be filled with good things, but has sent away [royal] the wealthy back empty-handed.
He has helped [royal] Israel the servant of His [royal], by remembering [royal] His mercy [royal],
according to what He [royal] has spoken [royal] to our ancestors — to Abraham and to his clan-descendants continuing forever.'
Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, then returned to her own home.
When the delivery time for Elizabeth was complete, she gave birth to a son.
Her neighbors and relatives heard that the Lord [royal] had shown [royal] great mercy [royal] to her, so they rejoiced-together with her.
It came to pass on the eighth day, they came to perform a circumcision ceremony for the child, and intended to name the child after his father, 'Zechariah.'
But the mother of the child answered, 'No, he must be named John.'
They said to her, 'There is no one among your [formal] relatives who has this name at all.'
So they made signs to ask the father of the child what to name the child.
Zechariah requested a writing-tablet, and then wrote, 'His name is John.' Everyone was amazed.
At that very moment, the mouth and tongue of Zechariah opened; he could speak, and he praised God.
Everyone who lived around the house was filled with reverent awe, and all these events were being talked about throughout the hill country of the region of Judea.
Everyone who heard kept this matter in their heart, saying, 'What kind of person will this child become?' For the hand [royal] of the Lord [royal] dwelt [royal] with him.
As for Zechariah the father of the child, he became filled with the Holy Spirit [royal] and prophesied, saying,
'Praise be to the Lord [royal] God of Israel, because He has visited [royal-come-visit] and has redeemed [royal] His people,
and has established [royal] a horn of salvation for us in the lineage of David the servant of His [royal],
according to what He [royal] has spoken [royal] through the mouth of His [royal] holy prophets from ancient times,
namely salvation to escape from our enemies and from the clutching-hand of all those who hate us,
in order to demonstrate [royal] His mercy [royal] to our ancestors, and to remember [royal] His holy covenant of His [royal],
namely the oath which He has sworn [royal] to Abraham our ancestor — that He would grant [royal] to us,
that when He had rescued [royal] us from the clutching-hand of enemies, we would be able to serve-devotedly [religious-service] Him [royal] without fear,
in holiness and righteousness before the face [royal] of Him [royal] throughout every day of our lives.
As for you, child-dear, you will be called a prophet of the Most High, because you will walk ahead before the face [royal] of the Lord [royal] to prepare His [royal] highway-paths,
to enable His people to know salvation, through the forgiveness of their sins,
by the tender mercy [royal] of our God, by which the rising-dawn from the heights will come-visit [royal] us,
to shine forth [royal] upon people who dwell in darkness and in the shadow of the Grim-Death, to lead [royal] our feet onto the way of peace.'
As for that baby, he grew up, became strong in spirit-soul, and dwelt in the wilderness until the day when he would publicly appear to Israel.
